slices.name: sha1_file
slices.code:
19 | def sha1_file(p: Path, chunk: int = 1024*1024) -> str:
  20 |     h = hashlib.sha1()
  21 |     try:
  22 |         with p.open("rb") as f:
  23 |             while True:
  24 |                 b = f.read(chunk)
  25 |                 if not b: break
  26 |                 h.update(b)
  27 |         return h.hexdigest()
  28 |     except PermissionError:
  29 |         return ""  # Skip files we can't read

slices.name: should_skip_dir
slices.code:
31 | def should_skip_dir(name: str, excludes: List[str]) -> bool:
  32 |     name_lower = name.lower()
  33 |     return any(name_lower == e.lower() for e in excludes)

slices.name: scan_root
slices.code:
35 | def scan_root(root: Path, include_exts: List[str], excludes: List[str], do_hash: bool) -> Iterable[Dict]:
  36 |     root = root.resolve()
  37 |     print(f"Scanning root: {root}")
  38 |     
  39 |     for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
  40 |         # prune excluded dirs in-place
  41 |         dirnames[:] = [d for d in dirnames if not should_skip_dir(d, excludes)]
  42 |         
  43 |         for fn in filenames:
  44 |             p = Path(dirpath) / fn
  45 |             ext = p.suffix.lower()
  46 |             
  47 |             # Extension filtering
  48 |             if include_exts and ext not in include_exts:
  49 |                 continue
  50 |             
  51 |             try:
  52 |                 stat = p.stat()
  53 |             except Exception:
  54 |                 continue
  55 |                 
  56 |             rec = {
  57 |                 "root": str(root),
  58 |                 "rel_path": str(p.relative_to(root)),
  59 |                 "abs_path": str(p),
  60 |                 "ext": ext,
  61 |                 "size": stat.st_size,
  62 |                 "mtime_iso": datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
  63 |             }
  64 |             
  65 |             # Hashing logic
  66 |             if do_hash and stat.st_size <= 50_000_000:  # avoid hashing giant files > 50MB
  67 |                 try:
  68 |                     rec["sha1"] = sha1_file(p)
  69 |                 except Exception:
  70 |                     rec["sha1"] = ""
  71 |             else:
  72 |                 rec["sha1"] = ""
  73 |                 
  74 |             yield rec

slices.name: main
slices.code:
76 | def main():
  77 |     ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Create a unified file map CSV.")
  78 |     # UPDATED: Arguments are no longer required.
  79 |     ap.add_argument("--roots", nargs="+", help="Directory roots to scan. Defaults to current directory.")
  80 |     ap.add_argument("--out", help="CSV path to write. Defaults to 'file_map.csv'.")
  81 |     ap.add_argument("--include-exts", default=",".join(DEFAULT_EXTS), help="Comma-separated extensions.")
  82 |     ap.add_argument("--exclude-dirs", default=",".join(DEFAULT_EXCLUDES), help="Comma-separated directories to exclude.")
  83 |     ap.add_argument("--hash", action="store_true", help="Compute SHA1 for files <=50MB.")
  84 |     
  85 |     args = ap.parse_args()
  86 | 
  87 |     # --- APPLY DEFAULTS ---
  88 |     # If no root provided, use current directory "."
  89 |     roots_input = args.roots if args.roots else ["."]
  90 |     # If no output provided, use "file_map.csv"
  91 |     out_path = args.out if args.out else "file_map.csv"
  92 | 
  93 |     include_exts = [e.strip() for e in args.include_exts.split(",") if e.strip()]
  94 |     include_exts = [e if e.startswith(".") else "."+e for e in include_exts]
  95 |     excludes = [e.strip() for e in args.exclude_dirs.split(",") if e.strip()]
  96 | 
  97 |     rows = []
  98 |     print(f"Starting scan...")
  99 |     print(f"Target Output: {Path(out_path).resolve()}")
 100 | 
 101 |     for r in roots_input:
 102 |         root = Path(r).expanduser()
 103 |         if not root.exists():
 104 |             print(f"[WARN] Root not found: {root}")
 105 |             continue
 106 |         for rec in scan_root(root, include_exts, excludes, args.hash):
 107 |             rows.append(rec)
 108 | 
 109 |     # Write to CSV
 110 |     try:
 111 |         out_p = Path(out_path)
 112 |         if out_p.parent.name: # Only try making directories if there is a parent path
 113 |             out_p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 114 |             
 115 |         with open(out_path, "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 116 |             fieldnames = ["root","rel_path","abs_path","ext","size","mtime_iso","sha1"]
 117 |             w = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
 118 |             w.writeheader()
 119 |             for r in rows:
 120 |                 w.writerow(r)
 121 | 
 122 |         print(f"Success! Wrote {len(rows)} rows to {out_path}")
 123 |         
 124 |     except PermissionError:
 125 |         print(f"[ERROR] Could not write to {out_path}. Is the file open in Excel?")
